title: eremophila glandulifera
intro: Eremophila glandulifera, also known as the Emu Bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its small, tubular flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eremophila glandulifera include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from colds and coughs, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. Eremophila glandulifera can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
